name: Alcorn, M. O.
description: Possesses that "sticking" ability which assures success. Takes his work very seriously and accomplishes much. Assists in the Chemistry laboratories.
text: Possesses that "sticking" ability which assures success. Takes his work very seriously and accomplishes much. Assists in the Chemistry laboratories.

name: Alcorn, Maurice L.
description: A hard worker—slow, but sure.
text: A hard worker—slow, but sure.

name: Allen, Glenn Luman
description: A good engineer—also takes considerable interest in his "soldiering" and is said to be Lieut. Boschen's pet (?).
text: A good engineer—also takes considerable interest in his "soldiering" and is said to be Lieut. Boschen's pet (?).

name: Brown, Robert Washington
description: Bob is an excellent student and a very deserving fellow. Amuses himself by writing peace-essays and participating in raids on Freshman dances. Will probably succeed Dr. Thomas.
text: Bob is an excellent student and a very deserving fellow. Amuses himself by writing peace-essays and participating in raids on Freshman dances. Will probably succeed Dr. Thomas.

name: Cabeen, Catherine Ahney
motto: "Thy voice ... is music to the ear, Soothing and soft, and gentle as the stream That strays mid summer flowers."
text: "Thy voice ... is music to the ear, Soothing and soft, and gentle as the stream That strays mid summer flowers."

name: Cantrell, Walter Thurman
description: The only "wireless" authority in the University. Pulls the "demon lightning" from out of the sky.
text: The only "wireless" authority in the University. Pulls the "demon lightning" from out of the sky.

name: Carroll, John Charles
clubs: First Prize ($50) in Essay Contest on Race Question, '15
First Prize Pi Beta Phi Essay in Economics, '14
description: Has always taken lots of interest in his class and the University, and has received a great many school honors as his reward.
text: Has always taken lots of interest in his class and the University, and has received a great many school honors as his reward.

name: Carolan, H. Clem
description: Clem is noted for his good nature. Doesn't say much but saws wood just the same.
text: Clem is noted for his good nature. Doesn't say much but saws wood just the same.

name: Coleman, Verna Mae
motto: "You know I haven't heard from Nelson this week. Ain't it fierce? Did you say this was exam week? Land, I'd forgotten all about it."
text: "You know I haven't heard from Nelson this week. Ain't it fierce? Did you say this was exam week? Land, I'd forgotten all about it."

name: Coker, Marion B.
description: Coker is a consistent worker, and the undisputed originator of "Short Circuits" in the Weekly.
text: Coker is a consistent worker, and the undisputed originator of "Short Circuits" in the Weekly.

name: Costen, James B.
motto: "Who is she, Jimmie?" "Why, Bo, she's a little cheer-leader in Ft. Smith High."
description: Is full of "pop", and delights in unfolding his line which invariably begins with "Bo".
text:
"Who is she, Jimmie?" "Why, Bo, she's a little cheer-leader in Ft. Smith High."
Is full of "pop", and delights in unfolding his line which invariably begins with "Bo".

name: Courson, William Hershia
clubs: Vice-President of Y. M. C. A.
description: Quiet and unassuming, and a good student. Is an important factor in the Y. M. C. A.
text: Quiet and unassuming, and a good student. Is an important factor in the Y. M. C. A.

name: Downs, Roy Richard
description: "Railroad" had to come back and tell his experiences. Praises the day on which the seniors adopted canes.
text: "Railroad" had to come back and tell his experiences. Praises the day on which the seniors adopted canes.

name: Dubs, Ford Harvey
description: Made his "A" in football in 1913. Insists that he was not named after "Henry". Rides his "put-put" machine during spare moments.
text: Made his "A" in football in 1913. Insists that he was not named after "Henry". Rides his "put-put" machine during spare moments.

name: Forrest, Grace
description: She would not have a man prosecuted for stealing her picture although it was set in gold; but if he carried off the setting and left the portrait, we would not answer for his safety.
text: She would not have a man prosecuted for stealing her picture although it was set in gold; but if he carried off the setting and left the portrait, we would not answer for his safety.

name: Gill, Thomas Tapscott
description: Got his degree in three years. Is a very precocious child, and a hard worker. Has rendered much valuable work to the University publications. Was known to go with a girl to the Skull and Torch banquet.
text: Got his degree in three years. Is a very precocious child, and a hard worker. Has rendered much valuable work to the University publications. Was known to go with a girl to the Skull and Torch banquet.

name: Greaves, Clifton D.
description: It is said that he never had a serious thought. An all-round "good-fellow", noted for his steady stream of wit and humor. Yes, it is true that all of it is not original with him, but what a memory!
text: It is said that he never had a serious thought. An all-round "good-fellow", noted for his steady stream of wit and humor. Yes, it is true that all of it is not original with him, but what a memory!

name: Harville, William E.
description: "Tuffy" has withstood the ravages of many serious love affairs since the beginning of his University career. Drowns his sorrows in the ecstasies of Spanish 1, and simply will not carry a cane.
text: "Tuffy" has withstood the ravages of many serious love affairs since the beginning of his University career. Drowns his sorrows in the ecstasies of Spanish 1, and simply will not carry a cane.

name: Higgs, Morton T.
description: "Mutt" holds the exalted position of Lord High Dictator to Freshmen with much dignity. Has that much-coveted faculty of "getting by" and rarely fails to do so.
text: "Mutt" holds the exalted position of Lord High Dictator to Freshmen with much dignity. Has that much-coveted faculty of "getting by" and rarely fails to do so.

name: Hilton, Esther Childs
description: Quiet, helpful and trustworthy, but potentially a deep-dyed criminal.
text: Quiet, helpful and trustworthy, but potentially a deep-dyed criminal.

name: Horton, Horace R.
description: Is a good engineer and deserving of much credit. Knows the ropes and pulls most of the wires.
text: Is a good engineer and deserving of much credit. Knows the ropes and pulls most of the wires.

name: Hinds, Helene Lois
description: Helene is always busy. She has longed for Dormitory life but she couldn't get rid of her family.
text: Helene is always busy. She has longed for Dormitory life but she couldn't get rid of her family.

name: Horton, William G.
description: He is gifted with a deep bass voice. Exults in attending class meetings, and is one of the main-stays of the M. E. department. Was given the name "Bush" before the new interpretation came into vogue.
text: He is gifted with a deep bass voice. Exults in attending class meetings, and is one of the main-stays of the M. E. department. Was given the name "Bush" before the new interpretation came into vogue.

name: Hunt, Ralph B.
description: Mike belongs to everything in school and has been an active worker in all of them. Is always willing to do his part, and has won many friends.
text: Mike belongs to everything in school and has been an active worker in all of them. Is always willing to do his part, and has won many friends.

name: King, Iler
motto: "There is strength deep buried in her heart."
description: She is full of fun, too, but since she is a new-comer only a few know her.
text:
"There is strength deep buried in her heart."
She is full of fun, too, but since she is a new-comer only a few know her.

name: Irby, Nolan M.
description: A scholar with little to say. Plays "500" as a means of diversion.
text: A scholar with little to say. Plays "500" as a means of diversion.

name: Land, Mildred
motto: "She liked whate'er she looked on and her looks went everywhere."
text: "She liked whate'er she looked on and her looks went everywhere."

name: Lee, Lucas
description: Although he is usually seeking the "game", Luke invariably gets by.
text: Although he is usually seeking the "game", Luke invariably gets by.

name: Lee, Wendell D.
description: A winsome chap and not afraid of work.
text: A winsome chap and not afraid of work.

name: Lincoln, Adaline
description: In spite of her pedestal of learning, the sixteen-year-old prodigy, can succumb to a childish tendency to prattle and has an adolescent interest in cute boys. Got degree in 3 years.
text: In spite of her pedestal of learning, the sixteen-year-old prodigy, can succumb to a childish tendency to prattle and has an adolescent interest in cute boys. Got degree in 3 years.

name: Matthews, Ben B.
motto: "Fickleness" is his greatest fault.
description: Makes good grades and is somewhat of a debater.
text:
"Fickleness" is his greatest fault.
Makes good grades and is somewhat of a debater.

name: McBride, John Edward
description: They say "Moc" is crazy about the women, but of late is down on them. A chemistry major seldom seen around that building.
text: They say "Moc" is crazy about the women, but of late is down on them. A chemistry major seldom seen around that building.

name: McKinney, Ruth
description: Ruth is an enthusiast in college activities and in spite of her devotion to bugs and all things biological, is very human.
text: Ruth is an enthusiast in college activities and in spite of her devotion to bugs and all things biological, is very human.

name: McDonald, Louise
clubs: Treasurer of Y. W. C. A.
description: Her money-making schemes rival Wallingford's. She is destined to be a great financier. Has a weakness for sarcasm.
text: Her money-making schemes rival Wallingford's. She is destined to be a great financier. Has a weakness for sarcasm.

name: McConnell, Willard W.
clubs: Treasurer of Y. M. C. A.
description: Mac is not the kind to set the world on fire, but no doubt something will get scorched before he's through.
text: Mac is not the kind to set the world on fire, but no doubt something will get scorched before he's through.

name: Moore, Vaughn
clubs: Social Committee, Y. M. C. A.
description: Is possessed of that nagging persistency which usually accomplishes that which he desires. As for the women—well, V. H. has a girl in every port.
text: Is possessed of that nagging persistency which usually accomplishes that which he desires. As for the women—well, V. H. has a girl in every port.

name: Nunn, H. E.
description: Although small in stature, Henry has a large heart and a warm spot reserved therein for each of his many friends.
text: Although small in stature, Henry has a large heart and a warm spot reserved therein for each of his many friends.

name: Oliver, James W.
description: Takes great pride in making announcements in the Mess Hall, and has grown quite proficient in the art.
text: Takes great pride in making announcements in the Mess Hall, and has grown quite proficient in the art.

name: Payne, Weston
description: He is an all-round man in every sense of the word. He is of steady habits and is easy-going—rather silent at times, but always thinking.
text: He is an all-round man in every sense of the word. He is of steady habits and is easy-going—rather silent at times, but always thinking.

name: Rodgers, Eunice Loraine
description: She is a very capable little person and is a star especially when it comes to Argumentation. Her favorite color is Black.
text: She is a very capable little person and is a star especially when it comes to Argumentation. Her favorite color is Black.

name: Rosencrantz, Franklin C.
description: He is rarely ever seen around the campus and it's a mystery where he spends his time.
text: He is rarely ever seen around the campus and it's a mystery where he spends his time.

name: Saddler, William Paul
description: He lives on College Ave., but for some unknown reason prefers to spend his spare moments on Lafayette. Bill is very popular as is shown by the many things he belongs to.
text: He lives on College Ave., but for some unknown reason prefers to spend his spare moments on Lafayette. Bill is very popular as is shown by the many things he belongs to.

name: Sears, Eliza Ella
motto: "The best is yet to come." "Perseverance keeps honor bright."
text: "The best is yet to come." "Perseverance keeps honor bright."

name: Short, Gilbert Young
clubs: President of Y. M. C. A.
description: He has proved his ability by the efficient manner in which he has managed the Y. M. C. A.
text: He has proved his ability by the efficient manner in which he has managed the Y. M. C. A.

name: Smith, Oscar D.
description: "O. D." is not one who might well be called a speed-demon, but he digs, and consistently, too.
text: "O. D." is not one who might well be called a speed-demon, but he digs, and consistently, too.

name: Smith, Harold A.
description: He is a Chemistry fiend of the first water. His popularity is evidenced by the number of pins he wears and the number of offices he has held.
text: He is a Chemistry fiend of the first water. His popularity is evidenced by the number of pins he wears and the number of offices he has held.

name: Snyder, Bryan, Jr.
description: "Dutch" is a very level-headed fellow, and possesses considerable business ability.
text: "Dutch" is a very level-headed fellow, and possesses considerable business ability.

name: Taylor, Irene Olcott
description: She is a literary critic, poet, and dramatist, and is destined to be a movie actress unless the Ben Greets get her first. She has a warm heart and a jolly laugh.
text: She is a literary critic, poet, and dramatist, and is destined to be a movie actress unless the Ben Greets get her first. She has a warm heart and a jolly laugh.

name: Thomas, Alvin N.
description: Alvin conducts all his affairs with becoming dignity. Takes an active interest in student affairs and has many friends—especially since he has had charge of the "Sticks."
text: Alvin conducts all his affairs with becoming dignity. Takes an active interest in student affairs and has many friends—especially since he has had charge of the "Sticks."

name: Wells, George C.
description: He is that taciturn man with a big smile. They say he is in love but it is hard to believe. He is a big bug in the E. E. department.
text: He is that taciturn man with a big smile. They say he is in love but it is hard to believe. He is a big bug in the E. E. department.

name: Winn, James A.
description: More than once have we been moved to tears at one moment, and laughter the next by those excellent speeches of his. He is a conscientious and fair-minded fellow, as well as a good student.
text: More than once have we been moved to tears at one moment, and laughter the next by those excellent speeches of his. He is a conscientious and fair-minded fellow, as well as a good student.

name: Knock, Elmo
description: He said that he was not a woman and he refused to put on a cap and gown. That made us curious to see how he would look in one so we had a picture drawn.
text: He said that he was not a woman and he refused to put on a cap and gown. That made us curious to see how he would look in one so we had a picture drawn.

name: Rice, P. X.
description: He knew that if he had a photograph made all his friends would want one, so we made a portrait of him. It is surprising how little change may take place in a human being in the long period of twenty years. At the age of three the subject looks very promising, but how disappointed his fond relatives must be now—in his looks of course, for Mack has really made quite a record at the University.
text: He knew that if he had a photograph made all his friends would want one, so we made a portrait of him. It is surprising how little change may take place in a human being in the long period of twenty years. At the age of three the subject looks very promising, but how disappointed his fond relatives must be now—in his looks of course, for Mack has really made quite a record at the University.

name: Alright, Chester
description: She found a quotation for every other girl in the class but she could not find one that she thought would do for herself.
text: She found a quotation for every other girl in the class but she could not find one that she thought would do for herself.

name: Brown, Hazel
motto: "Bid me discourse and I will enchant thine ear."
text: "Bid me discourse and I will enchant thine ear."

name: Cabler, Cleveland
description: Swollen with pride. A good orator and one of the Varsity debaters against Oklahoma.
text: Swollen with pride. A good orator and one of the Varsity debaters against Oklahoma.

name: Campbell, W. P.
description: The pride of the ladies. He made such a hit in his gold braid that he had no trouble finding his battalion maid.
text: The pride of the ladies. He made such a hit in his gold braid that he had no trouble finding his battalion maid.

name: Cheever, E. H.
description: For many years a banker, but still quite young.
text: For many years a banker, but still quite young.

name: Banks, L. D.
description: A newcomer among us, but already quite popular.
text: A newcomer among us, but already quite popular.

name: Cochran, M. W.
description: Maurice has found a rival. Who? Paul.
text: Maurice has found a rival. Who? Paul.

name: Conner, Christine
motto: "Thinking is but an idle waste of thought."
text: "Thinking is but an idle waste of thought."

name: Coffey, Jewel
motto: "Fluency of speech is seldom of real use except to spread out a small idea over a large space."
text: "Fluency of speech is seldom of real use except to spread out a small idea over a large space."

name: Daniels, J. B.
description: The beautiful boy from Dermott.
text: The beautiful boy from Dermott.

name: Ferguson, Christelle
description: "Exceedingly well read." This is her first year among us, but she has already distinguished herself.
text: "Exceedingly well read." This is her first year among us, but she has already distinguished herself.

name: Von Jagersfeld, Evelyn
motto: "That makes me so mad!"
text: "That makes me so mad!"

name: Fisher, M. F.
description: Silver-tongued orator of the junior class. The one who started the measles.
text: Silver-tongued orator of the junior class. The one who started the measles.

name: Gilmore, Lucile
motto: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild."
text: "Of manners gentle, of affections mild."

name: Henson, J. A.
description: A gentleman of leisure, sure, but he always gets by.
text: A gentleman of leisure, sure, but he always gets by.

name: Harrell, T. L.
motto: "The world will be what it will be, so what is the use to worry."
text: "The world will be what it will be, so what is the use to worry."

name: Horner, John
description: Agri, orator, good-fellow, and ladies' man, all combined; and quite a combination it is.
text: Agri, orator, good-fellow, and ladies' man, all combined; and quite a combination it is.

name: Wilkinson, Margaret
motto: "She is the very pineapple of politeness."
description: "Oh my, I should say so! But ain't it awful?"
text:
"She is the very pineapple of politeness."
"Oh my, I should say so! But ain't it awful?"

name: Illing, Leo M. H.
description: The fifty thousand dollar kid. A new-comer at the U.
text: The fifty thousand dollar kid. A new-comer at the U.

name: Jenkins, Catherine
motto: "Charms strike the heart, but merit wins the soul."
text: "Charms strike the heart, but merit wins the soul."

name: Richardson, C. O.
description: No, "R" doesn't come between "J" and "K" in our alphabet, but Richardson can be stuck in just anywhere.
text: No, "R" doesn't come between "J" and "K" in our alphabet, but Richardson can be stuck in just anywhere.

name: Kitchens, C. E.
description: A great doctor of medicine. Family physician for Gray Hall.
text: A great doctor of medicine. Family physician for Gray Hall.

name: Markle, Eva
motto: "Her voice was ever soft, Gentle and low, an excellent thing in woman."
text: "Her voice was ever soft, Gentle and low, an excellent thing in woman."

name: Ward, Zora
description: Always wrapped in some deep thought.
text: Always wrapped in some deep thought.

name: Myers, C. B.
motto: "These dog-gone merchants wouldn't give you an ad on a bet."
description: That classical looking gentleman with glasses. He is always willing to help.
text:
"These dog-gone merchants wouldn't give you an ad on a bet."
That classical looking gentleman with glasses. He is always willing to help.

name: Mendenhall, Ruby
motto: "Neat—not gaudy."
text: "Neat—not gaudy."

name: McGaughy, J. B.
motto: "Fate made me what I am."
text: "Fate made me what I am."

name: Merrill, N. D.
description: He is not a fish but a chemistry shark.
text: He is not a fish but a chemistry shark.

name: Milburn, J. B.
description: He bears the colors, and truly, there was never a more faithful sergeant.
text: He bears the colors, and truly, there was never a more faithful sergeant.

name: Moore, J. I.
motto: "I love the name of Mary."
description: The little Lieutenant. Many a maiden has cast her eyes toward him.
text:
"I love the name of Mary."
The little Lieutenant. Many a maiden has cast her eyes toward him.

name: Mitchell, E. E.
description: Little, but all there.; There are just too many Mitchells in school.
text: Little, but all there.; There are just too many Mitchells in school.

name: McCarthy, N. A.
description: He is not found wanting when he is needed.
text: He is not found wanting when he is needed.

name: Overton, Minnie
motto: "Reason's whole pleasure, all the joys of sense, Lie in three words, health, peace and competence."
text: "Reason's whole pleasure, all the joys of sense, Lie in three words, health, peace and competence."

name: Polk, Mary
motto: "'Tis well to be merry and wise, 'Tis well to be honest and true, And pleasant, too, to think on."
text: "'Tis well to be merry and wise, 'Tis well to be honest and true, And pleasant, too, to think on."

name: Pape, F. D.
description: Was never seen without a co-ed. He engineered the Engineers' representation in The Razorback.
text: Was never seen without a co-ed. He engineered the Engineers' representation in The Razorback.

name: Palmer, R. C.
description: A man with a judgment all his own.
text: A man with a judgment all his own.

name: Quaile, Beatrix
motto: "I am the very pink of propriety."
text: "I am the very pink of propriety."

name: Rawlings, A. J.
description: He won his fame in the Fayetteville High School and Gray Hall.
text: He won his fame in the Fayetteville High School and Gray Hall.

name: Scarlet, W. C.
description: Not as mature as his name might indicate.
text: Not as mature as his name might indicate.

name: Seward, Marguerite
motto: "The mildest manners and the gentlest heart."
text: "The mildest manners and the gentlest heart."

name: Sims, C. D.
description: He drills when he is there. The good-looking little man.
text: He drills when he is there. The good-looking little man.

name: Smith, Carr
description: The little man with the rosy cheeks. He bugles the soldiers home.
text: The little man with the rosy cheeks. He bugles the soldiers home.

name: Stuart, J. E.
description: The one who thought the junior men should carry canes in order to compete with the seniors.
text: The one who thought the junior men should carry canes in order to compete with the seniors.

name: Stuart, G. B.
description: He is quiet and retired and is well-liked by the ladies.
text: He is quiet and retired and is well-liked by the ladies.

name: Walkup, R. W.
description: As his name implies, he walks up the hill every night.
text: As his name implies, he walks up the hill every night.

name: Willey, George
description: The master of ceremonies at the freshman reception.
text: The master of ceremonies at the freshman reception.

name: Willis, Robert B.
description: A wise man is the one who seeketh after matrimony while he is yet good looking.
text: A wise man is the one who seeketh after matrimony while he is yet good looking.

name: Eichelberger, O. H.
description: The last junior to have his picture made for The Razorback and also the one with the most complicated name.; The short end of the military department.
text: The last junior to have his picture made for The Razorback and also the one with the most complicated name.; The short end of the military department.

name: Amis, J. W.
description: The lamb of the flock.
text: The lamb of the flock.

name: Ashley, Louise
motto: "If one could have that little head of hers."
text: "If one could have that little head of hers."

name: Ballard, Eva
motto: "Love seldom haunts the breast where learning lies."
text: "Love seldom haunts the breast where learning lies."

name: Bain, J. O.
description: Terror of the gridiron.
text: Terror of the gridiron.

name: Buechley, Florence
motto: "Hope the best for yourself."
text: "Hope the best for yourself."

name: Adams, J. D.
motto: "His smile is adored by all the ladies."
text: "His smile is adored by all the ladies."

name: Bond, G. W.
description: Just pegs along without much to say but accomplishes much.
text: Just pegs along without much to say but accomplishes much.

name: Brewster, W. R.
description: We bid thee an affectionate farewell.
text: We bid thee an affectionate farewell.

name: Buchanan, Henrietta
motto: "As full of fun, of frolic, as any I have seen."
text: "As full of fun, of frolic, as any I have seen."

name: Campbell, Kate
motto: "Simplicity and truth swell in her breast."
text: "Simplicity and truth swell in her breast."

name: Carroll, J. J.
description: Talks less, does more. Believes in freshmen knowing their places.
text: Talks less, does more. Believes in freshmen knowing their places.

name: Callahan, Jean
motto: "To all she is polite without parade."
text: "To all she is polite without parade."

name: Casey, J. E.
description: Build upon a rock if you want a sound foundation.
text: Build upon a rock if you want a sound foundation.

name: Church, M. A.
description: No, he is not a master of arts; those are only his initials.
text: No, he is not a master of arts; those are only his initials.

name: Coffield, H. A.
motto: "Get out of that mob!"
description: The leader of the Gray Hall gang.
text:
"Get out of that mob!"
The leader of the Gray Hall gang.

name: Cherry, Robert
description: He eats math three times a day and enjoys it.
text: He eats math three times a day and enjoys it.

name: Cherry, R. L.
description: The Chemistry shark.
text: The Chemistry shark.

name: Cook, Edwina
motto: "Why all this toil for triumph of an hour?"
text: "Why all this toil for triumph of an hour?"

name: Clardy, R. K.
description: No one knows what the other half is.
text: No one knows what the other half is.

name: Douthit, Jesse
description: The Soph with the perpetual grin.
text: The Soph with the perpetual grin.

name: Dowell, Gladys
motto: "Come and trip it as you go, On the light fantastic toe."
text: "Come and trip it as you go, On the light fantastic toe."

name: Fish, R. J.
description: And he could sit by a stream all day and never get a nibble.
text: And he could sit by a stream all day and never get a nibble.

name: Harris, R. D.
description: The girls all say.
text: The girls all say.

name: Hamby, W. B.
description: The star orator of Hill Hall.
text: The star orator of Hill Hall.

name: Henderson, C. A.
description: Contentment is written all over his face.
text: Contentment is written all over his face.

name: Smith, Norman
description: A common name but an uncommon kid.
text: A common name but an uncommon kid.

name: Irby, Guy
description: The dignity of his bearing is easily recognizable.
text: The dignity of his bearing is easily recognizable.

name: Jordan, Kara
motto: "Of study took the most heed and care."
text: "Of study took the most heed and care."

name: King, Ann
motto: "She is the girl we all admire, For lofty ambitions we find none higher."
text: "She is the girl we all admire, For lofty ambitions we find none higher."

name: Klausmeier, Ruth
motto: "The height of my ambition is only to find my place."
text: "The height of my ambition is only to find my place."

name: Lanier, John
description: A modest man, a truly darling boy.
text: A modest man, a truly darling boy.

name: Leitzell, Velma
motto: "Anything for a quiet life."
text: "Anything for a quiet life."

name: Forgy, G. H.
description: The Soph with the political future.
text: The Soph with the political future.

name: Lockharte, Jane Dorothe Isabel
motto: "Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor’s tablets, nor her ink, nor her pens—just take 'em."
text: "Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor’s tablets, nor her ink, nor her pens—just take 'em."

name: Leach, L. O.
description: Solid as a brick wall, and admired by all. Has been to Kansas.
text: Solid as a brick wall, and admired by all. Has been to Kansas.

name: Mcdonald, Dorothy
motto: "Popular with both boys and girls."
text: "Popular with both boys and girls."

name: Mcdonald, C. P.
motto: "Who am I that I should do this?"
text: "Who am I that I should do this?"

name: Mcateer, J. T.
description: "Hey, John, here’s a freshman needs a strapping."
text: "Hey, John, here’s a freshman needs a strapping."

name: Massey, Beal
description: The memory lingers.
text: The memory lingers.

name: Mcilroy, Mertye
motto: "A little learning is a dangerous thing."
text: "A little learning is a dangerous thing."

name: Meadows, C. T.
description: The star member of the Gray Hall Uplift Society.
text: The star member of the Gray Hall Uplift Society.

name: Middlebrooks, Pearl
motto: "Excellence is the reward of labor."
text: "Excellence is the reward of labor."

name: Melton, Joe
description: A star in Economics and Dr. Waterman’s pet.
text: A star in Economics and Dr. Waterman’s pet.

name: Milburn, Bryan
description: The man with the strong rich voice who made it hot for Oklahoma.
text: The man with the strong rich voice who made it hot for Oklahoma.

name: Moncrief, P. D.
description: The man responsible for all these knocks, but big enough to defend himself.
text: The man responsible for all these knocks, but big enough to defend himself.

name: Mitchell, W. M.
description: A sophomore at the first pop. Level-headed and well-liked.
text: A sophomore at the first pop. Level-headed and well-liked.

name: Morgan, R. E.
description: Dealer in belts.
text: Dealer in belts.

name: Nelson, W. E.
description: Innocence is portrayed in every feature.
text: Innocence is portrayed in every feature.

name: Norwood, Ellen
motto: "She attracts me daily with her gentle virtues, so soft and beautiful and heavenly."
text: "She attracts me daily with her gentle virtues, so soft and beautiful and heavenly."

name: Overstreet, Elizabeth
motto: "And that sweet dignity all who saw admired."
text: "And that sweet dignity all who saw admired."

name: Pendleton, H. F.
description: Has many times been mistaken for a senior.
text: Has many times been mistaken for a senior.

name: Parker, E. L.
description: One of the finer works of art.
text: One of the finer works of art.

name: Perkins, H. E.
motto: "When I ope my lips all dogs bark."
text: "When I ope my lips all dogs bark."

name: Peden, Orchid
description: The prim little girl who doesn't like to smile. Speaks to the boys semi-annually.
text: The prim little girl who doesn't like to smile. Speaks to the boys semi-annually.

name: Ramsey, Adele
motto: "I chatter, chatter as I go, to one and then another. Some talk fast and some talk slow, but I talk on forever."
text: "I chatter, chatter as I go, to one and then another. Some talk fast and some talk slow, but I talk on forever."

name: Rainwater, Sloan
motto: "I can do it if it can be done."
text: "I can do it if it can be done."

name: Reeves, Ruth
motto: "Greatness does not consist of much talking."
text: "Greatness does not consist of much talking."

name: Russell, Nona
description: One of the few lucky ones of our number, very studious.
text: One of the few lucky ones of our number, very studious.

name: Reichart, Chris
description: The Razorbacks' hefty center.
text: The Razorbacks' hefty center.

name: Sailor, Lela
motto: "Her heart and hand both open and free, for what she has she gives."
text: "Her heart and hand both open and free, for what she has she gives."

name: Sanford, Bess
motto: "She says little but knows much."
text: "She says little but knows much."

name: Scott, Louise
motto: "Of rather short duration, but an interesting specimen."
text: "Of rather short duration, but an interesting specimen."

name: Shifflett, J. J.
description: A man of much experience.
text: A man of much experience.

name: Shadrach, W. S.
description: A very fast man—in football.
text: A very fast man—in football.

name: Shinn, Jarvis
description: He never says too much.
text: He never says too much.

name: Simms, Lucie
motto: "Why should I blush to own I love."
text: "Why should I blush to own I love."

name: Smith, May
motto: "My words are half in earnest, half in jest."
text: "My words are half in earnest, half in jest."

name: Sour, S. S.
motto: "Ich weiß nicht."
text: "Ich weiß nicht."

name: Sullivan, Harry
description: That mild, meek blond with the prize-fighter's name.
text: That mild, meek blond with the prize-fighter's name.

name: Torrence, Julia
motto: "A diamond in the ring of acquaintance."
text: "A diamond in the ring of acquaintance."

name: Tarver, V. X.
description: A draftsman from Star City.
text: A draftsman from Star City.

name: Vineyard, Marion
motto: "She is of so free, so kind, so apt, so blessed a disposition."
text: "She is of so free, so kind, so apt, so blessed a disposition."

name: Vineyard, Mittie
motto: "Draw aside the drapery of gloom and let the sunshine chase the clouds away."
text: "Draw aside the drapery of gloom and let the sunshine chase the clouds away."

name: Volentine, Opie
motto: "What is life without a fair companion?"
text: "What is life without a fair companion?"

name: Wachter, Virginia
motto: "Be ashamed to catch yourself idle."
text: "Be ashamed to catch yourself idle."

name: White, Carita
motto: "To keep my health! to do my work! to live! That's all I ask."
text: "To keep my health! to do my work! to live! That's all I ask."

name: Woolf, Cora
description: Very meek and gentle. Although she is a wolf there is no danger.
text: Very meek and gentle. Although she is a wolf there is no danger.

name: Mcdaniel, V. B.
description: The ceaseless chatter of the harmless. An admirer of blond(s).
text: The ceaseless chatter of the harmless. An admirer of blond(s).

name: Zoll, A. A.
description: He is "there" on the gridiron, in politics and any place you put him.
text: He is "there" on the gridiron, in politics and any place you put him.

name: Christopher, F.
description: Star half-back of the reserves.
text: Star half-back of the reserves.

name: Mullins, W. E.
description: Yes, he is a sophomore, all right. He has said so all the year.
text: Yes, he is a sophomore, all right. He has said so all the year.
